content: Also you said it was from the Resonance sheet, and I remember it being associated with Horla, but it doesn't seem to be the name of any of Horla's Resonances. <:noellehuh:950648966851231804> hold on lemme check something else
okay, I guess I'll just follow the CN as closely as I can, and if the literal TL is too mundane I'll add some dramatic flair?
it's part of chapter 1's gameplay explo gimmick, as in the Aigis chapter
oh. huh.
interesting.

If it helps, I put my EN TL of the Resonances on the back burner because of the announcement that Ch. 8 & 9 are changing soon, but everything up to that is well done, so I can give you that real quick if you'd like
sure, that'd be nice to cross reference with
Here, this should have everything relevant in it
Lens and I also retranslated "Innocent Return Gift" to "An Innocent Thanks", and I can dig up that conversation real quick to see if it's relevant to your language/culture or not
I was there for the convo actually!
I think "Return Gift" is okay for Indonesian
I've been told the innocence is important
welp
lol
lol Innocent is actually pretty hard to TL in a way that sounds good imo
but yeah whatever you think is best!
fair lol
it's more like the childlike aspect right? not the guiltless one?
in this case I mean
would 'pure' be an okay substitute?
I believe the original meaning also was adjacent to naïveté
but that's pretty much all the information I can give <:shrug:1003188954218123314>
hmm, I think pure is sort of adjacent? like people think of children as pure right
pure-hearted, absolutely

I kinda want to discuss the proper translation of the name of the third Mythag founder, Klaraksch/Kral-Kerkesh/whatever
Logic dictates that his proper name should be **Klarkash-Ton**, the name Lovecraft (referenced as his own self-insert Ward Phillips) gave to his friend and protege **Clark Ashton** Smith (for context, Lovecraft oft came up with sort of Mythos-grimoire-author-sonas for his writer friends as an in-joke and a way to pay respect).
But apparently the "-Ton" aspect isn't present in the game in any language, most notably in the phonetic CN transcription? Should we, then, use **Klarkash**?
Also, I'm unsure about the "-Ton", just my dilettante observations. Maybe the name should be used as-is, if I failed to deduce the presence of the elusive "-Ton"
Ace has actually elaborated on that specific case before in the official discord: https://discord.com/channels/1131791637933199470/1326453763355185215/1429678235708555424
so the correct name is Khlarcus

Thanks, a question answered for sure
Now I wonder what'd take more time, trying to search through the official Discord's history in case it was elusively documented somewhere or researching the phonetics, lmao
珍贵的花香 should mean "precious floral scent" instead of "Petal's Last Breath" right?
bc the current EN TL is the latter which completely different from the actual chinese meaning unless it's some kind of metaphor/slang that I'm not getting

The English TL has some intentional artistic liberties taken by the devs here and there, so it's likely this is an example of that. We've been told ***not*** to re-translate these from CN -> EN (as in, EN should *keep* "Petal's Last Breath"), but I'm confident in saying you can probably choose whichever meaning you prefer when translating to another language. <:BennettThumbsUp:773345984474120202>
most volunteers are translating from EN anyway so either or should be totally fine
I see, so it's just artistic liberty for the other languages?

personally, if I was in that position, I'd pick whichever of the 2 meanings would create a more prosaic statement in the target language, but in general **we should always try to stick as close as we reasonably can to the source material**, whether that be the CN source or the EN source.
<@88644018673582080> and I are notoriously on opposite ends of the artistic liberties spectrum, but he's objectively more correct, so
tbh I do think Petal's Last Breath is more dramatic but I actually don't know if that's a good thing lol
it's from the resonance gameplay source sheet
but imo it's pretty strange to have 2 completely different meanings in the first place

The devs have made it very clear that their strong suit as developers is flavor (or drama, to use your words), and personally my stance is that the work we do should be as far from bland as reasonably possible, so I encourage prose and esoterica
like I get if they're reasonably close or like, evoke the same vibe but in this case I think they're not even the same phrase lol
for example there's another one where the EN is "Oneiric Remembrance" and the CN means "memories in dreams" which is close-ish
that's fine
but it's totally different for Petal's Last Breath and "precious floral scent" lol

idk, I kinda see it
Floral -> Petal (of a flower)
Scent -> Breath
Precious -> Last (as the last of something is usually precious)
so the (floral) Petal's (precious) Last Breath (has a scent)
I'm stupid though, so I could be completely off
hmm, that does make sense, although it's quite a few associations to get there haha
